CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-28-2022 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-22-2021 Continued To Feb-15-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiffs Ingrid De Leon Rodas And Jose Avila'S Motion For Attorney Fees And Expenses
Off Calendar Oct-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Deepak Devabose, Esq. (310.552.2250) of Knight Law Group for Plaintiffs.
Doug Proudfoot, Esq. (408.623.4479; dproudfoot@g3pmlaw.com) of Gates, Gonter, Guy, Proudfoot & Muench LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs Ingrid De Leon Rodas And Jose Avila'S Motion For Attorney Fees And Expenses:

Plaintiffs' motion for attorney fees, costs and expenses is granted in the amount of $87,419. Buyers who prevail on California lemon law claims recover attorney fees, costs and expenses "reasonably incurred." (Civ. Code 1794(d).) Hourly rates sought for plaintiffs' counsels' work are reasonable by San Francisco standards. However, at least $1,230 was spent on an unsuccessful fraud claim not brought under the lemon law; that reduces the lodestar fee amount to $58,358. (See Akins v. Enterprise Rent-A-Car Co. (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 1127, 1133.) Plaintiffs seek a .5 lodestar enhancement. I reduce that to .3, because lemon law practice is highly routinized and because much risk was effectively eliminated when defendants offered to repurchase plaintiffs' vehicle as suit was being filed. Costs and expenses of $11,554 were unopposed.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-04-2020 Continued To Jan-11-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-27-2019 Set For Trial On Aug-31-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-30-2019 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. GEORGE T. AGUILAR, ESQ. of Knight Law Group LLP (Tel: 310-552-2250) appearing via CourtCall for the plaintiffs;
Atty. JOHN FAULCONER, ESQ. of Lewis Brisbois (Tel: 703-862-5550) appearing for defendants FCA US LLC and Michael Stead's Auto Depot, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS FCA US LLC AND MICHAEL STEAD'S AUTO DEPOT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants FCA US LLC and Michael Stead's Auto Depot, Inc.'s demurrer to the fourth cause of action for fraudulent inducement/concealment is sustained without leave to amend. The economic loss rule bars the claim. Plaintiffs allege that Defendants concealed certain defects in the electrical architecture of their vehicles, but does not allege that their vehicle was affected by any of the problems allegedly caused by those electrical defects, nor do they plead that they suffered any damages other than the alleged economic damage related to the purchase of the vehicle itself. The defect of which Plaintiffs complain was a transmission/shifting problem, not an electrical problem. (Compl. 175-178.) Plaintiffs also fail to plead any facts showing that she was exposed to liability for personal damages independent of the economic loss. "The economic loss rule requires a purchaser to recover in contract for purely economic loss due to disappointed expectations, unless he can demonstrate harm above and beyond a broken contractual promise." (Robinson Helicopter Co., Inc. v. Dana Corp. (2004) 34 Cal.4th 979, 988; Food Safety net Services v. Eco Safe Systems USA, Inc. (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 1118, 1132 [economic loss rule barred fraud claim based on falsehoods that were not conceptually distinct from the contract].) Plaintiffs' reliance on the "narrow" exception to the rule in Robinson Helicopter Co. is misplaced, as the Court's holding in that case was "limited to a defendant's affirmative misrepresentations on which a plaintiff relies and which expose a plaintiff to liability for personal damages independent of the plaintiff's economic loss." (34 Cal.4th at 993.) As Plaintiffs concede, they do not allege that Defendants made any affirmative misrepresentations.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185, E-MAIL: tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)
